title: Case 2 — the gene list argues with the marrow
demonstrates: Output 1 doing its real job. The clinician wrote MDS. An NPM1 mutation at 38% VAF says otherwise — and WHO and ICC don't even agree with each other about what to call it. The assistant must show the three-way fork and let the clinician choose, not pick a side.
clinical_problem: Eight months of macrocytic anaemia, now transfusion-dependent at roughly 3-weekly intervals. Referred by GP after a failed trial of B12 and folate. No prior cytotoxic therapy.
film: Dysplastic neutrophils with hypogranular cytoplasm and pseudo-Pelger-Huet forms. Occasional circulating blast. Macrocytosis, anisopoikilocytosis.
marrow_diagnosis: Myelodysplastic syndrome with excess blasts (MDS-EB2). Blasts 12%. Trilineage dysplasia. Karyotype: 46,XY [20] — normal.
